name: add_board_outline
description: Draw the PCB board outline (Edge.Cuts layer) as a rectangle, rounded rectangle, circle or polygon.

x.description: X coordinate of top-left corner for rectangles (default: 0)

y.description: Y coordinate of top-left corner for rectangles (default: 0)

name: add_board_text
description: Add a text label to a PCB layer (e.g. silkscreen, fab, courtyard).

position.description: Position of the text

layer.description: Layer to place the text on

name: add_component_3d_model
description: Attach a 3D model to one or more PLACED footprints on the open board, live via the KiCAD IPC API (changes appear instantly, no file conflicts). Use this for components already on the PCB; for a library .kicad_mod use add_footprint_3d_model.

reference.description: Footprint reference(s), e.g. 'D1', ['D1','D2'], or '*' for all footprints

replace.description: Replace an existing model with the same filename (default true)

name: add_component_annotation
description: Add a text annotation or comment to a PCB component.

annotation.description: Annotation or comment text to add

visible.description: Whether the annotation should be visible on the PCB

name: add_copper_pour
description: Add a copper pour (ground/power plane) to the PCB

outline.description: Array of {x, y} points defining the pour boundary. If omitted, the board outline is used.

name: add_footprint_3d_model
description: Attach (or replace) a 3D model — .step/.stp/.wrl — to a .kicad_mod footprint file. KiCAD path variables like ${KIPRJMOD} or ${KICAD10_3DMODEL_DIR} are supported. Use this after create_footprint so the part shows up in the 3D viewer (Alt+3).

footprintPath.description: Full path to the .kicad_mod file, e.g. C:/MyLib.pretty/MyPart.kicad_mod

modelPath.description: Path to the 3D model file. Prefer ${KIPRJMOD}/MyProj.3dshapes/MyPart.step for portability.

replace.description: Replace an existing model with the same filename (default true)

name: add_gnd_stitching_vias
description: Drop GND stitching vias across the board with collision checking against every non-GND segment, via, and pad on every copper layer (PTH vias penetrate the full stackup, so missing any one layer is the classic silent-short failure mode). Three combinable strategies: `grid` (regular grid across the interior), `around_refs` (densify around named ICs), and `in_zones` (only place vias inside an actual GND copper zone). Supports `dryRun` to preview placements without writing.

gndNet.description: Name of the ground net (default: auto-detect GND / GROUND / VSS / /GND).

strategies.description: Which placement strategies to combine (default: ['grid']). Pass ['grid', 'around_refs', 'in_zones'] for full coverage.

viaDrill.description: Via drill diameter in mm (default 0.3). Must be smaller than viaSize.

clearance.description: Extra clearance beyond required between each new via and existing copper, in mm (default 0.2).

spacing.description: Grid spacing in mm for `grid` and `around_refs` strategies (default 5.0).

densifyRefs.description: Reference designators to densify ground around (used by `around_refs`). Targets: MCUs, switching regulators, RF parts.

densifyRadius.description: How many grid cells around each ref to try (default 2 = 5x5 candidate field per ref).

edgeMargin.description: Keep-out from the board edge in mm (default 0.5).

maxVias.description: Cap on total placements across all strategies (default unlimited).

dryRun.description: If true, return the placements that would be made but don't modify the board (default false).

name: add_hierarchical_sheet
description: Insert a hierarchical-sheet reference block into a parent schematic, pointing at an existing sub-sheet file. Adds the sheet box, name/file fields, a sheet_instances path entry on the next page number, and fixes sub-sheet component instance paths so ERC resolves references.

subsheetPath.description: Path to the existing sub-sheet .kicad_sch to reference

position.description: Top-left of the sheet box in mm (default 50,50)

name: add_layer
description: Add a new copper or technical layer to the PCB stackup.

position.description: Layer position

name: add_library_symbol_property
description: Add or update a custom property (Manufacturer, MPN, LCSC, etc.) on a symbol definition in the lib_symbols section. This makes the property available to all instances of that symbol in the schematic.

position.description: Position {x, y} in mm (default: 0, 0)

name: add_mounting_hole
description: Place a mounting hole (NPTH or PTH) at the specified position on the PCB.

position.description: Position of the mounting hole

padDiameter.description: Optional diameter of the pad around the hole

name: add_net
description: Create a new net on the PCB

name: add_net_class
description: Create a named net class with specific design rules (clearance, track width, via size) and assign nets to it.

description.description: Optional description of the net class

trackWidth.description: Track width for this net class (mm)

viaDiameter.description: Via diameter for this net class (mm)

viaDrill.description: Via drill size for this net class (mm)

uvia_diameter.description: Micro via diameter for this net class (mm)

uvia_drill.description: Micro via drill size for this net class (mm)

diff_pair_width.description: Differential pair width for this net class (mm)

diff_pair_gap.description: Differential pair gap for this net class (mm)

nets.description: Array of net names to assign to this class

name: add_no_connect
description: Add a no-connect flag (X marker) to a pin that is intentionally left unconnected. This suppresses ERC 'Pin not connected' errors for unused pins. PREFERRED: supply componentRef + pinNumber to snap to the exact pin endpoint. Alternatively supply position [x, y] in mm matching the pin endpoint exactly.

position.description: Position [x, y] in mm. Required when componentRef/pinNumber are not given.

componentRef.description: Component reference to snap to (e.g. U1, R1). Use with pinNumber.

pinNumber.description: Pin number or name on componentRef (e.g. '1', 'GND'). Use with componentRef.

name: add_schematic_component
description: Add a component to the schematic. Symbol format is 'Library:SymbolName' (e.g., 'Device:R', 'EDA-MCP:ESP32-C3')

position.description: Position on schematic

unit.description: Unit number for multi-unit symbols (1=A, 2=B, 3=C, …). Defaults to 1.

angle.description: Rotation angle in degrees (KiCad CCW). 0=vertical resistor, 90=horizontal. Defaults to 0.

mirrorY.description: Mirror the symbol horizontally (flip left-right). Useful for transistors facing opposite direction.

name: add_schematic_hierarchical_label
description: Add a hierarchical label (sheet interface port) to a sub-sheet schematic. Hierarchical labels are the connection points that link a sub-sheet to its parent via sheet pins. The label text must exactly match the corresponding sheet pin name.

text.description: Label text (e.g. 'SD_CLK') — must match the sheet pin name

position.description: Position [x, y] in mm

shape.description: Signal direction from the sub-sheet's perspective

orientation.description: Rotation in degrees: 0=label points right, 180=label points left (default: 0)

name: add_schematic_net_label
description: Add a net label to the schematic. PREFERRED: supply componentRef + pinNumber to snap the label to the exact pin endpoint — this guarantees an electrical connection. Alternatively supply position [x, y], but the coordinates must match the pin endpoint exactly (even a 0.01 mm offset breaks the connection). The response includes actual_position (coordinates actually used) and snapped_to_pin (present when a pin reference was resolved).

netName.description: Name of the net (e.g., VCC, GND, SIGNAL_1)

position.description: Position [x, y] for the label. Required when componentRef/pinNumber are not given.

componentRef.description: Component reference to snap label to (e.g. U1, R1). Use with pinNumber.

pinNumber.description: Pin number or name on componentRef to snap label to (e.g. '1', 'GND'). Use with componentRef.

name: add_schematic_text
description: Add a free-form text annotation to the schematic. Use this to add notes, labels, section headings, or documentation strings directly on the schematic canvas. Unlike net labels, text annotations have no electrical significance.

position.description: Position [x, y] in schematic mm coordinates

name: add_schematic_wire
description: Draws a wire on the schematic between two or more coordinate points. Always call get_schematic_pin_locations first to get the approximate pin coordinates, then pass them as the first and last waypoints. snapToPins (on by default) will correct any float imprecision by snapping endpoints to the exact nearest pin coordinate. To route around components, add intermediate waypoints between the start and end: e.g. [[x1,y1], [xMid,y1], [xMid,y2], [x2,y2]] routes horizontally then vertically. Intermediate waypoints are never snapped.

waypoints.description: Ordered list of [x, y] coordinates. Minimum 2 points.

snapToPins.description: Snap the first and last waypoints to the nearest pin (default: true)

name: add_sheet_pin
description: Add a pin to a sheet symbol block on the parent schematic. Sheet pins are the parent-side connection points that correspond to hierarchical labels in the sub-sheet. The pinName must exactly match a hierarchical_label in the sub-sheet.

sheetName.description: Sheet name as it appears in the Sheetname property (e.g. 'Storage')

pinName.description: Pin name — must match a hierarchical_label in the sub-sheet

pinType.description: Signal direction (should match the sub-sheet hierarchical label shape)

position.description: Pin position [x, y] in mm — must be on the sheet block boundary

orientation.description: Pin orientation: 0=right edge of sheet box, 180=left edge (default: 0)

name: add_symbol_property
description: Add or update a custom property (Manufacturer, MPN, LCSC, etc.) on a symbol in a .kicad_sym library file.

position.description: Position {x, y} in mm (default: 0, 0)

name: add_via
description: Add a via to the PCB

position.description: Via position

name: add_zone
description: Create a copper fill zone (pour) on a PCB layer for a specified net.

name: align_components
description: Align multiple PCB components horizontally, vertically or on a grid with optional spacing.

references.description: Array of component references to align

name: annotate_schematic
description: Assign reference designators to unannotated components (R? → R1, R2, ...). Must be called before tools that require known references.

name: assign_net_to_class
description: Assign a net to an existing net class to apply its specific design rules.

name: autoplace_schematic_fields
description: Automatically reposition every component's Reference and Value field so they sit outside the component body AND outside any net labels attached to its pins, avoiding collisions with other components and already-placed fields. Like KiCAD's built-in field auto-placement but net-label aware. Optionally limit to specific references.

references.description: Only reposition these references (default: all components)

clearance.description: Gap in mm between the body/label extent and field text (default one 1.27mm grid unit)

name: autoroute
description: Run Freerouting autorouter on the current PCB. Exports to Specctra DSN, runs Freerouting CLI, and imports the routed SES result. Requires Java 11+ and freerouting.jar (see check_freerouting). Set `attempts` > 1 to run best-of-N: Freerouting is invoked multiple times with varied `--max-passes`, each result is scored by (nets_routed * 1000 + segments, +50000 bonus when every `targetNets` entry routed), and the winning SES is imported. Single-attempt behaviour is unchanged when `attempts` is omitted.

freeroutingJar.description: Path to freerouting.jar (default: ~/.kicad-mcp/freerouting.jar or FREEROUTING_JAR env)

maxPasses.description: Maximum routing passes for single-attempt mode (default: 20). Ignored when `attempts` > 1; use `passSchedule` instead.

attempts.description: Number of Freerouting runs to try (default: 1 — backward-compatible). When > 1, runs best-of-N: scores each attempt by routing completeness and keeps the SES with the highest score. Recommended: 3–5 for dense boards.

targetNets.description: Optional list of critical net names. An attempt that routes all of them earns a 50,000-point scoring bonus, breaking ties in favour of designs that include the must-have nets.

passSchedule.description: Per-attempt `--max-passes` values to cycle through (default: [50, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80, 85, 90, 55, 95]). The list wraps if `attempts` exceeds its length.

name: batch_add_and_connect
description: Place multiple components AND wire their nets in a single call — the fewest-round-trip way to build a subcircuit. Each component is like batch_add_components plus an optional 'nets' map {pin: netName}. Components are placed first, then nets are connected via batch_connect.

nets.description: Map of pin -> netName to label after placement

name: batch_add_components
description: Add multiple components to a schematic in one call (far fewer round-trips than add_schematic_component). Each component: {symbol:'Library:Name', reference, value?, footprint?, position:{x,y}, rotation?, includePins?}. Reference/Value fields are auto-positioned outside the body (disable with auto_position_fields=false). Returns per-component snapped position, field positions, body_bbox, and an overall placement_bbox.

origin_x.description: X offset added to every component position (mm)

origin_y.description: Y offset added to every component position (mm)

auto_position_fields.description: Auto-place Ref/Value fields outside the body (default true)

name: batch_add_no_connects
description: Add no-connect (X) flags to multiple pins in one call, to mark intentionally unconnected pins and silence ERC. 'pins' is a list of {componentRef, pinName}.

name: batch_connect
description: Place net labels on multiple pins in one call to wire nets quickly. 'connections' is a map {reference: {pin: netName}} where pin is a number or name. If a facing label for the same net is nearby, a wire is drawn instead of a duplicate label. Set replace=true to clear any existing label at a pin first. Warns about power nets missing a PWR_FLAG.

replace.description: Delete existing labels at each pin before placing (default false)

name: batch_edit_schematic_components
description: Edit multiple existing components in one call. 'components' is a map {reference: {value?, footprint?, newReference?, ...}}; each entry is applied via the single-component editor. Returns per-reference updated/errors.

components.description: Map of reference -> fields to change

name: batch_list_symbol_pins
description: Return pin names, numbers, types, and symbol-local coordinates for multiple symbols in a single call. Use instead of calling list_symbol_pins repeatedly when placing a subcircuit — saves 5–10 round-trips. Each result includes pins (with x/y/angle in symbol-local coords, Y-up per KiCAD lib convention) and body_bbox (bounding box of pin envelope ±1.27mm, symbol-local coords). IMPORTANT: coordinates are symbol-local (Y-up, pre-rotation); after placement use get_schematic_pin_locations for post-rotation schematic coordinates. Set compact=true for simple 2-pin passives (Device:R/C/L) to get just pin_count, body_bbox, and is_symmetric.

schematicPath.description: Path to .kicad_sch — enables project-local sym-lib-table lookup

compact.description: If true, omit per-pin detail for standard 2-pin symmetric passives.

name: batch_set_schematic_property_positions
description: Move many Reference/Value field labels in a single file read/write — far faster than repeated set_schematic_property_position calls. Pass an 'updates' array; each item is {reference, property:'Reference'|'Value', x, y, angle?, visible?}. Returns per-item applied/failed lists.

updates.description: List of field moves to apply

name: check_clearance
description: Check the actual clearance between two PCB items (track, via, pad, zone or component) and report whether it meets the design rules.

id.description: ID of the first item (if applicable)

position.description: Position to check (if ID not provided)

id.description: ID of the second item (if applicable)

position.description: Position to check (if ID not provided)

name: check_courtyard_overlaps
description: Detect courtyard overlaps between footprints and (optionally) flag courtyards that extend past the board outline. Accepts a `positions` dict of hypothetical placements so an AI can validate a proposed move_component / place_component before committing it. Returns overlap pairs with intersection extents (mm) and per-component boundary violations.

positions.description: Virtual placements: map of reference designator to [x, y] or [x, y, rotation_degrees] in mm. Each listed ref is checked AS IF it were at the given coordinates. Unspecified refs use their current board position.

refs.description: Limit the check to these refs (default: every footprint on the board).

margin.description: Extra clearance in mm added around every courtyard (default 0). Useful to enforce a manufacturing keepout wider than the symbol's declared courtyard.

include_boundary.description: Also flag courtyards that extend past the board outline (default true).

board_outline.description: Optional board outline bbox override. Default: derived from Edge.Cuts.

name: check_freerouting
description: Check if Java and Freerouting JAR are available on the system. Run this before autoroute to verify prerequisites.

name: check_kicad_ui
description: Check if KiCAD UI is currently running

name: close_project
description: Close the currently loaded KiCAD project: optionally save, then drop the in-memory board and clear session state. Use this to hand control back so the user (or the agent) can edit project files directly without the MCP later clobbering those changes on save.

save.description: Save the board to disk before closing (default true). If false and there are unsaved changes, the close proceeds but the response warns they were discarded.

name: connect_passthrough
description: Connects all pins of a source connector (e.g. J1) to matching pins of a target connector (e.g. J2) via shared net labels — pin N gets net '{netPrefix}_{N}'. Use this for FFC/ribbon cable passthrough adapters instead of calling connect_to_net for every pin.

netPrefix.description: Net name prefix, e.g. 'CSI' → CSI_1, CSI_2 (default: PIN)

pinOffset.description: Add to pin number when building net name (default: 0)

name: connect_to_net
description: Connect a component pin to a named net by adding a wire stub and net label at the exact pin endpoint. The response includes pin_location (exact pin coords), label_location (where the label was placed), and wire_stub (the wire segment added) so you can confirm the placement.

netName.description: Name of the net to connect to

name: copy_routing_pattern
description: Copy routing pattern (traces and vias) from a group of source components to a matching group of target components. The offset is calculated automatically from the position difference between the first source and first target component. Useful for replicating routing between identical circuit blocks.

targetRefs.description: References of the target components in same order as sourceRefs (e.g. ['U2', 'R2', 'C2'])

traceWidth.description: Override trace width in mm (default: keep original width)

name: create_footprint
description: Create a new KiCAD footprint (.kicad_mod) inside a .pretty library directory. Supports SMD and THT pads, courtyard, silkscreen, and fab-layer rectangles.

libraryPath.description: Path to the .pretty library directory (created if missing). E.g. C:/MyProject/MyLib.pretty

shape.description: Pad shape (default: rect for SMD, circle for THT)

roundrect_ratio.description: Corner radius ratio for roundrect shape (0.0–0.5, default 0.25)

pads.description: List of pads to add (can be empty for outlines-only footprints)

courtyard.description: Courtyard rectangle on F.CrtYd (recommended: 0.25 mm clearance around pads)

fabLayer.description: Fab-layer rectangle on F.Fab (shows component body)

name: create_hierarchical_subsheet
description: Create a new sub-sheet .kicad_sch file and link it into a parent schematic in a single call (create_schematic + add_hierarchical_sheet). The fastest way to grow a hierarchical design.

subsheetPath.description: Path for the new sub-sheet .kicad_sch to create

metadata.description: Optional metadata for the new sub-sheet (title, etc.)

name: create_netclass
description: Create a new net class with custom design rules.

name: create_project
description: Create a new KiCAD project

name: create_schematic
description: Create a new schematic

name: create_symbol
description:
Create a new schematic symbol in a .kicad_sym library file (created if missing). After creation, use register_symbol_library so KiCAD finds it. Pin positions are where the wire connects; the symbol body is drawn between them.

Coordinate tips:
- Body rectangle typically spans ±2.54 to ±5.08 mm
- Pins on left side: at.x = body_left - length, angle=0 (wire goes right)
- Pins on right side: at.x = body_right + length, angle=180 (wire goes left)
- Pins on top: at.y = body_top + length, angle=270 (wire goes down)
- Pins on bottom: at.y = body_bottom - length, angle=90 (wire goes up)
- Standard pin length: 2.54 mm, standard grid: 2.54 mm

libraryPath.description: Path to the .kicad_sym file (created if missing)

onBoard.description: Include in netlist for PCB (default true)

angle.description: Direction the pin wire extends FROM the symbol body: 0=right, 90=up, 180=left, 270=down

at.description: Pin endpoint position (where the wire connects)

pins.description: List of pins (can be empty for graphical-only symbols)

rectangles.description: Body rectangle(s). Typically one rectangle defining the IC body.

points.description: List of XY points in mm

polylines.description: Polyline graphics for custom body shapes (op-amp triangles, etc.)

overwrite.description: Replace existing symbol with same name (default false)

name: delete_component
description: Remove a component from the PCB by its reference designator.

reference.description: Reference designator of the component to delete (e.g., 'R5')

name: delete_schematic_component
description:
Remove a placed symbol from a KiCAD schematic (.kicad_sch).

This removes the symbol instance (the placed component) from the schematic.
It does NOT remove the symbol definition from lib_symbols.

Note: This tool operates on schematic files (.kicad_sch).
To remove a footprint from a PCB, use delete_component instead.

reference.description: Reference designator of the component to remove (e.g. R1, U3)

name: delete_schematic_net_label
description: Remove a net label from the schematic.

netName.description: Name of the net label to remove

position.description: Position to disambiguate if multiple labels with same name

name: delete_schematic_wire
description: Remove a wire from the schematic by start and end coordinates.

name: delete_symbol
description: Remove a symbol from a .kicad_sym library file.

name: delete_trace
description: Delete traces from the PCB. Can delete by UUID, position, or bulk-delete all traces on a net.

traceUuid.description: UUID of a specific trace to delete

position.description: Delete trace nearest to this position

net.description: Delete all traces on this net (bulk delete)

layer.description: Filter by layer when using net-based deletion

name: download_jlcpcb_database
description:
Download the JLCPCB parts catalog to a local SQLite database for fast offline search.

Sources (no API credentials required by default):
  - cdfer (default): in-stock subset (~600k parts, ~1.5 GB download). Single file,
    no extra tools — the most reliable path, especially on Windows.
  - yaqwsx: the FULL catalog (all parts incl. out-of-stock, ~10 GB extracted).
    Use source="yaqwsx" if you specifically want everything. Requires a 7z CLI.
  - official: official JLCPCB API, used only if JLCPCB_APP_ID/JLCPCB_API_KEY/
    JLCPCB_API_SECRET are set.

One-time setup; downloads resume automatically if interrupted. Re-run with
force=true to refresh.

force.description: Force re-download even if database exists

source.description: Force one source. "cdfer" (default) = in-stock subset, no 7z needed. "yaqwsx" = FULL ~10GB catalog (needs a 7z CLI). "official" = JLCPCB API (needs creds).

name: duplicate_component
description: Duplicate an existing PCB component at an offset position, optionally with a new reference designator.

name: edit_component
description: Edit properties of an existing PCB component (reference, value, footprint).

name: edit_footprint_pad
description: Edit an existing pad inside a .kicad_mod footprint file. Updates size, position, drill, or shape without recreating the whole footprint.

footprintPath.description: Full path to the .kicad_mod file, e.g. C:/MyLib.pretty/R_Custom.kicad_mod

padNumber.description: Pad number to edit, e.g. '1' or 2

name: edit_schematic_component
description:
Update properties of a placed symbol in a KiCAD schematic (.kicad_sch) in-place.

Use this tool to:
  • assign or update the footprint, value, or reference designator,
  • reposition field labels (Reference / Value text),
  • add, update, or remove ARBITRARY CUSTOM PROPERTIES used by BOM and sourcing
    workflows: MPN, Manufacturer, Manufacturer_PN, Distributor, DigiKey, DigiKey_PN,
    Mouser_PN, LCSC, JLCPCB_PN, Voltage, Tolerance, Power, Dielectric, etc.

Custom properties are first-class — they survive ERC, are exported by export_bom,
and are picked up by the JLCPCB / Digi-Key BOM tooling. Newly-added properties
default to hidden so they do not clutter the schematic canvas.

Multiple updates can be batched in a single call: pass any combination of
`footprint`, `value`, `newReference`, `fieldPositions`, `properties`,
and `removeProperties` together.

This is more efficient than delete + re-add because it preserves the component's
position and UUID. Operates on .kicad_sch files only — to modify a PCB footprint
use edit_component instead.

reference.description: Current reference designator of the component (e.g. R1, U3)

newReference.description: Rename the reference designator (e.g. R1 → R10)

justify.description: Text justification: "left", "right", "center", "top", "bottom", or combined "left top" / "right bottom". Array form ["left", "top"] is also accepted. Omit to leave the existing justify unchanged. Pass "center" to reset to the KiCad default (removes the justify directive).

fieldPositions.description: Reposition field labels: map of field name to {x, y, angle, justify?} (e.g. {"Reference": {"x": 12.5, "y": 17.0, "justify": "left"}})

x.description: Label X position in mm (default: component X)

y.description: Label Y position in mm (default: component Y)

hide.description: Whether to hide the property text on the schematic. Defaults to true for newly-created custom properties (BOM/sourcing data is normally hidden).

fontSize.description: Font size in mm for the label (default: 1.27)

properties.description: Add or update component properties. Map of property name to either a string value (sensible defaults) or a full spec object {value, x?, y?, angle?, hide?, fontSize?}. Use this to attach BOM and sourcing metadata such as MPN, Manufacturer, Distributor, DigiKey, LCSC, JLCPCB_PN, Voltage, Tolerance, Dielectric, Power, etc. Built-in fields (Reference, Value, Footprint, Datasheet) can also be set this way but the dedicated parameters above are clearer. Example: {"MPN": "RC0603FR-0710KL", "Manufacturer": "Yageo", "Tolerance": "1%"}

removeProperties.description: List of custom property names to delete from this component. The built-in fields Reference, Value, Footprint, and Datasheet cannot be removed (clear them by setting value to "" instead). Example: ["OldMPN", "Distributor_PN"]

name: enrich_datasheets
description:
Fill in missing Datasheet URLs in a KiCAD schematic using LCSC part numbers.

For every placed symbol that has:
  • (property "LCSC" "C123456") set
  • (property "Datasheet" "~") or empty

Sets the Datasheet field to:
  https://www.lcsc.com/datasheet/C123456.pdf

The URL is then visible in KiCAD's footprint browser, symbol properties dialog,
and any tool that reads the standard KiCAD Datasheet field.
No API key or internet lookup required – the URL is constructed directly.

Use dry_run=true to preview changes without writing.

schematic_path.description: Path to the .kicad_sch file to enrich

dry_run.description: If true, show what would be changed without writing to disk (default: false)

name: export_3d
description: Export the PCB as a 3D model (STEP, STL, VRML or OBJ) including optional copper, solder mask, silkscreen and component 3D models.

outputPath.description: Path to save the 3D model file

name: export_3d_cli
description: Export a 3D model of the PCB via kicad-cli. The `format` param selects the subcommand (step, glb, stl, ply, brep, xao, vrml); only flags valid for that subcommand are forwarded. STEP/glb/stl/ply/brep/xao share the geometry/include flag set (no-board-body, include-tracks/pads/zones/inner-copper/silkscreen/soldermask, fuse-shapes, fill-all-vias, component/net filters, min-distance, etc.; no-optimize-step is STEP-only); vrml uses units + models-dir/models-relative instead. Rich CLI sibling of export_3d and export_vrml. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output 3D model file path

noUnspecified.description: Exclude 3D models for components with 'Unspecified' footprint type

noDnp.description: Exclude 3D models for components with 'Do not populate' attribute

userOrigin.description: User-specified output origin e.g. '1x1in', '25.4x25.4mm' (default unit mm)

gridOrigin.description: Use Grid Origin for output origin

drillOrigin.description: Use Drill Origin for output origin

substModels.description: Substitute STEP/IGS models in place of VRML models

boardOnly.description: Only generate a board with no components

cutViasInBody.description: Cut via holes in board body even if conductor layers not exported

componentFilter.description: Only include component models matching this refdes list (comma, wildcards)

includeInnerCopper.description: Export elements on inner copper layers

includeSilkscreen.description: Export silkscreen graphics as flat faces

includeSoldermask.description: Export soldermask layers as flat faces

fillAllVias.description: Don't cut via holes in conductor layers

minDistance.description: Min distance between points to treat as separate (default '0.01mm')

netFilter.description: Only include copper items belonging to nets matching this wildcard

noOptimizeStep.description: Do not optimize STEP file (enables writing parametric curves; STEP only)

modelsDir.description: Folder to store 3D models in (VRML only; empty = embed in main file)

modelsRelative.description: Use relative model paths with modelsDir (VRML only)

name: export_bom
description: Export a Bill of Materials (BOM) from the PCB in CSV, XML, HTML or JSON format.

outputPath.description: Path to save the BOM file

groupByValue.description: Whether to group components by value

includeAttributes.description: Optional array of additional attributes to include

name: export_drill
description: Generate drill files for a PCB via kicad-cli, exposing the full Excellon/Gerber drill option set (format, drill origin, zero suppression, oval format, units, mirror-Y, minimal header, separate PTH/NPTH files, drill map + map format). Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the drill files

excellonSeparateTh.description: Generate independent files for NPTH and PTH holes

generateMap.description: Generate a drill map / summary file

mapFormat.description: Drill map format when generateMap is set (default pdf)

gerberPrecision.description: Gerber coordinate precision (5 or 6) when format=gerber

name: export_dsn
description: Export the current PCB to Specctra DSN format. Useful for manual Freerouting workflow or external autorouters.

outputPath.description: Output DSN file path (default: same dir as board)

name: export_gencad
description: Export the PCB in GenCAD format via kicad-cli. Assembly/test interchange format. Exposes padstack flip, unique pin/footprint shape generation, drill-file origin, and store-origin-coordinate options. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output .cad file path

uniqueFootprints.description: Generate a new shape for each footprint instance (do not reuse shapes)

useDrillOrigin.description: Use drill/place file origin as origin

storeOriginCoord.description: Save the origin coordinates in the file

name: export_gerber
description: Export PCB Gerber manufacturing files to a directory. Optionally include drill files, map files and choose layer subset.

layers.description: Optional array of layer names to export (default: all)

useProtelExtensions.description: Whether to use Protel filename extensions

generateMapFile.description: Whether to generate a map file

useAuxOrigin.description: Whether to use auxiliary axis as origin

name: export_gerber_single
description: Plot the given layers to a SINGLE Gerber file via kicad-cli (`pcb export gerber`). Singular sibling of export_gerbers. Exposes the full single-file Plot option set (X2, netlist attributes, DNP fab-layer modes, soldermask subtraction, aperture macros, drill-file origin, precision, Protel extension). Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output Gerber file path

layers.description: Layers to plot, untranslated names e.g. ['F.Cu','B.Cu','Edge.Cuts']

commonLayers.description: Layers to include on every plot (e.g. ['Edge.Cuts'])

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

includeBorderTitle.description: Include the border and title block

sketchPadsOnFabLayers.description: Draw pad outlines and numbers on fab layers

hideDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Don't plot DNP footprint text/graphics on fab layers

sketchDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot DNP footprints in sketch mode on fab layers

crossoutDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot an 'X' over DNP footprint courtyards and strike out their refdes

noX2.description: Do not use the extended X2 Gerber format

noProtelExt.description: Use KiCad Gerber file extensions instead of Protel

name: export_gerbers
description: Plot Gerber files for a PCB via kicad-cli, exposing the full Plot-dialog option set (X2, netlist attributes, DNP handling, soldermask subtraction, precision, drill-file origin, stored board plot settings, etc). Reads the board from disk, so it reflects the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the Gerber files

layers.description: Layers to plot, untranslated names e.g. ['F.Cu','B.Cu','Edge.Cuts']

commonLayers.description: Layers to include on every plot (e.g. ['Edge.Cuts'])

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

sketchPadsOnFabLayers.description: Draw pad outlines and numbers on fab layers

hideDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Don't plot DNP footprint text/graphics on fab layers

sketchDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot DNP footprints in sketch mode on fab layers

crossoutDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot an 'X' over DNP footprint courtyards and strike out their refdes

noX2.description: Do not use the extended X2 Gerber format

noProtelExt.description: Use KiCad Gerber file extensions instead of Protel

boardPlotParams.description: Use the Gerber plot settings already stored in the board file

name: export_ipc2581
description: Export the PCB in IPC-2581 format via kicad-cli. Single-file MES/CAD interchange carrying placement, nets and BOM part data inline. The bomCol* params map schematic fields to the embedded BOM columns (e.g. internal P/N, manufacturer P/N) — useful for assembly/MES imports. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output .xml file path

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

bomColIntId.description: Schematic field to use for the BOM Internal Id column

bomColMfgPn.description: Schematic field to use for the BOM Manufacturer Part Number column

bomColMfg.description: Schematic field to use for the BOM Manufacturer column

bomColDistPn.description: Schematic field to use for the BOM Distributor Part Number column

bomColDist.description: Value to insert into the BOM Distributor column

name: export_ipcd356
description: Generate an IPC-D-356 bare-board electrical-test netlist via kicad-cli. Consumed by flying-probe and bed-of-nails testers. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output .ipc / netlist file path

name: export_netlist
description: Export the schematic netlist to a file using kicad-cli. Supports KiCad XML (default), Spice (for simulation), Cadstar, and OrcadPCB2 formats. Use this when you need to write a netlist file to disk — for example to produce a SPICE file for simulation or to diff against a reference. To get net/component data inline without writing a file, use generate_netlist instead.

schematicPath.description: Absolute path to the .kicad_sch schematic file

outputPath.description: Absolute path for the output file (e.g. /tmp/design.spice)

name: export_odb
description: Export the PCB in ODB++ format via kicad-cli. Single job archive (copper, drill, placement, components, nets, outline) widely used by CAM/MES/assembly. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output file path (archive or directory per compression)

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

name: export_pcb_dxf
description: Plot the PCB layout to DXF via kicad-cli, exposing the full layer-plot option set (layer + common-layer lists, refdes/value exclusion, soldermask subtraction, use-contours, use-drill-origin, border+title, output units, DNP fab-layer modes, drill shape, single/multi output modes). Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output DXF file path (or directory in multi mode)

layers.description: Layers to plot, untranslated names e.g. ['F.Cu','B.Cu','Edge.Cuts']

commonLayers.description: Layers to include on every plot (e.g. ['Edge.Cuts'])

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

sketchPadsOnFabLayers.description: Draw pad outlines and numbers on fab layers

hideDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Don't plot DNP footprint text/graphics on fab layers

sketchDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot DNP footprints in sketch mode on fab layers

crossoutDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot an 'X' over DNP footprint courtyards and strike out their refdes

useContours.description: Plot graphic items using their contours

useDrillOrigin.description: Plot using the drill/place file origin

includeBorderTitle.description: Include the border and title block

drillShapeOpt.description: Pad/via drill shape option (0 none, 1 small, 2 actual; default 2)

modeSingle.description: Single file; output path is full path; LAYER_LIST controls all layers

modeMulti.description: Multi output; output path is a directory (GUI-like plotting)

name: export_pcb_pdf
description: Plot the PCB layout to PDF via kicad-cli, exposing the full layer-plot option set (layer + common-layer lists, mirror, refdes/value exclusion, border+title, soldermask subtraction, DNP fab-layer modes, negative, black-and-white, theme, drill shape, and single/separate/multipage output modes). Rich CLI sibling of export_pdf. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output PDF file path (or directory in separate mode)

layers.description: Layers to plot, untranslated names e.g. ['F.Cu','B.Cu','Edge.Cuts']

commonLayers.description: Layers to include on every plot (e.g. ['Edge.Cuts'])

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

mirror.description: Mirror the board (to show bottom layers)

includeBorderTitle.description: Include the border and title block

sketchPadsOnFabLayers.description: Draw pad outlines and numbers on fab layers

hideDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Don't plot DNP footprint text/graphics on fab layers

sketchDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot DNP footprints in sketch mode on fab layers

crossoutDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot an 'X' over DNP footprint courtyards and strike out their refdes

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: PCB editor settings)

drillShapeOpt.description: Pad/via drill shape option (0 none, 1 small, 2 actual; default 2)

modeSingle.description: Single file; output path is full path; LAYER_LIST controls all layers

modeSeparate.description: Plot the layers to individual PDF files

modeMultipage.description: Plot the layers to a single multi-page PDF

name: export_pcb_svg
description: Plot the PCB layout to SVG via kicad-cli, exposing the full layer-plot option set (layer + common-layer lists, mirror, soldermask subtraction, negative, black-and-white, theme, DNP fab-layer modes, page-size mode, fit-page-to-board, exclude-drawing-sheet, drill shape, single/multi output modes). Rich CLI sibling of export_svg. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output SVG file path (or directory in multi mode)

layers.description: Layers to plot, untranslated names e.g. ['F.Cu','B.Cu','Edge.Cuts']

commonLayers.description: Layers to include on every plot (e.g. ['Edge.Cuts'])

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

mirror.description: Mirror the board (to show bottom layers)

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: PCB editor settings)

sketchPadsOnFabLayers.description: Draw pad outlines and numbers on fab layers

hideDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Don't plot DNP footprint text/graphics on fab layers

sketchDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot DNP footprints in sketch mode on fab layers

crossoutDnpFootprintsOnFabLayers.description: Plot an 'X' over DNP footprint courtyards and strike out their refdes

pageSizeMode.description: Page sizing mode (0 frame+title block, 1 current page size, 2 board area only)

drillShapeOpt.description: Pad/via drill shape option (0 none, 1 small, 2 actual; default 2)

modeSingle.description: Single file; output path is full path; LAYER_LIST controls all layers
