name: export_pdf
description: Export the PCB layout as a PDF document, optionally selecting layers, page size and colour mode.

outputPath.description: Path to save the PDF file

layers.description: Optional array of layer names to include (default: all)

blackAndWhite.description: Whether to export in black and white

name: export_pos
description: Generate a component placement (position / pick-and-place) file via kicad-cli, exposing the full CLI option set (side, format, units, bottom-negate-X, drill-file origin, SMD-only, exclude through-hole / DNP, gerber board edge). Rich CLI sibling of export_position_file. Reads the last SAVED state of the .kicad_pcb.

outputPath.description: Output position file path

side.description: Board side (gerber format only supports front or back; default both)

units.description: Output units; ascii or csv format only (default in)

bottomNegateX.description: Use negative X coordinates for bottom-layer footprints (ascii/csv only)

excludeFpTh.description: Exclude all footprints with through-hole pads (ascii/csv only)

excludeDnp.description: Exclude all footprints with the Do Not Populate flag set

name: export_position_file
description: Export a component placement/position file (pick-and-place) for PCB assembly in CSV or ASCII format.

outputPath.description: Path to save the position file

side.description: Which board side to include (default: both)

name: export_sch_bom
description: Generate a Bill of Materials from a schematic via kicad-cli (`sch export bom`), exposing the full option set (presets, field/label lists, grouping, sorting, filtering, DNP/excluded-from-BOM handling, and field/string/ref delimiters). schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputPath.description: Output BOM file path

preset.description: Named BOM preset from the schematic, e.g. 'Grouped By Value'

formatPreset.description: Named BOM format preset from the schematic, e.g. 'CSV'

fields.description: Ordered comma list of fields to export (supports special substitutions)

labels.description: Ordered comma list of labels to apply to the exported fields

groupBy.description: Fields to group references by when field values match

sortField.description: Field name to sort by (default Reference)

filter.description: Filter string to remove output lines

refRangeDelimiter.description: Character for reference ranges; blank disables ranges (default '-')

keepTabs.description: Keep tab characters from input fields

keepLineBreaks.description: Keep line break characters from input fields

name: export_sch_dxf
description: Export a schematic to DXF via kicad-cli (`sch export dxf`), one DXF per page into a directory. Exposes drawing-sheet override, theme, black-and-white, exclude-drawing-sheet, default-font, and page selection. schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the DXF files

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: schematic settings)

pages.description: Comma list of page numbers to print (blank = all pages)

name: export_sch_hpgl
description: Export a schematic to HPGL via kicad-cli (`sch export hpgl`), one plot per page into a directory. Exposes drawing-sheet override, exclude-drawing-sheet, default-font, page selection, pen size, and the origin/scale mode. schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the HPGL files

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

pages.description: Comma list of page numbers to print (blank = all pages)

origin.description: Origin and scale: 0 bottom left, 1 centered, 2 page fit, 3 content fit

name: export_sch_pdf
description: Export a schematic to PDF via kicad-cli (`sch export pdf`), exposing the full option set (drawing-sheet override, theme, black-and-white, exclude-drawing-sheet, default-font, the PDF property-popup / hierarchical-link / metadata excludes, no-background-color, and page selection). schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputPath.description: Output PDF file path

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: schematic settings)

excludePdfPropertyPopups.description: Do not generate property popups in PDF

excludePdfHierarchicalLinks.description: Do not generate clickable links for hierarchical elements in PDF

excludePdfMetadata.description: Do not generate PDF metadata from AUTHOR and SUBJECT variables

noBackgroundColor.description: Avoid setting a background color (regardless of theme)

pages.description: Comma list of page numbers to print (blank = all pages)

name: export_sch_ps
description: Export a schematic to PostScript via kicad-cli (`sch export ps`), one PS per page into a directory. Exposes drawing-sheet override, theme, black-and-white, exclude-drawing-sheet, default-font, no-background-color, and page selection. schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the PostScript files

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: schematic settings)

noBackgroundColor.description: Avoid setting a background color (regardless of theme)

pages.description: Comma list of page numbers to print (blank = all pages)

name: export_sch_python_bom
description: Export the legacy Python-BOM intermediate XML from a schematic via kicad-cli (`sch export python-bom`). This is the XML netlist consumed by the schematic editor's Python BOM scripts. Minimal option set (output + input only). schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputPath.description: Output XML file path

name: export_sch_svg
description: Export a schematic to SVG via kicad-cli (`sch export svg`), one SVG per page into a directory. Exposes drawing-sheet override, theme, black-and-white, exclude-drawing-sheet, default-font, no-background-color, and page selection. schematicPath is REQUIRED.

outputDir.description: Output directory for the SVG files

drawingSheet.description: Path to a drawing sheet override

theme.description: Color theme to use (default: schematic settings)

noBackgroundColor.description: Avoid setting a background color (regardless of theme)

pages.description: Comma list of page numbers to print (blank = all pages)

name: export_schematic_pdf
description: Export schematic to PDF format using kicad-cli.

outputPath.description: Output PDF file path

name: export_schematic_svg
description: Export schematic to SVG format using kicad-cli.

outputPath.description: Output SVG file path

name: export_svg
description: Export the PCB layout as an SVG vector image, optionally selecting layers and colour mode.

outputPath.description: Path to save the SVG file

layers.description: Optional array of layer names to include (default: all)

blackAndWhite.description: Whether to export in black and white

name: export_vrml
description: Export the PCB as a VRML 3D model for use in web viewers or simulation tools.

outputPath.description: Path to save the VRML file

useRelativePaths.description: Whether to use relative paths for 3D models

name: find_component
description: Search for a PCB component by reference designator or value and return its position and properties.

name: find_orphaned_wires
description: Find wire segments with at least one dangling endpoint — not connected to a component pin, net label, or another wire. Orphaned wires cause ERC 'wire end unconnected' errors. Does not require the KiCad UI to be running.

name: find_overlapping_elements
description: Detect spatially overlapping symbols, wires, and labels in the schematic. Finds duplicate power symbols at the same position, collinear overlapping wires, and labels stacked on top of each other.

tolerance.description: Distance threshold in mm for label proximity and wire collinearity checks. Symbol overlap uses bounding-box intersection. (default: 0.5)

name: find_wires_crossing_symbols
description: Find all wires that cross over component symbol bodies. Wires passing over symbols are unacceptable in schematics — they indicate routing mistakes where a wire was drawn across a component instead of around it.

name: generate_netlist
description: Return a structured JSON netlist from the schematic — component list (reference, value, footprint) and net list (net name with all connected component/pin pairs). Use this to inspect or verify connectivity within the conversation. Does not write any file. To export a netlist file in Spice, KiCad XML, Cadstar, or OrcadPCB2 format, use export_netlist instead.

schematicPath.description: Absolute path to the .kicad_sch schematic file

name: get_backend_state
description: Return the active backend, realtime status, loaded project/board paths, and dirty state.

name: get_board_2d_view
description: Render a 2D image of the PCB using kicad-cli. Returns PNG, JPG, or SVG. Use layers to filter — e.g. ["F.Cu","B.Cu","Edge.Cuts"] for copper + outline only. Use responseMode to choose delivery:   "inline" (default) — PNG/JPG rendered as an image visible to Claude; SVG returned as text.   "file" — image written next to the .kicad_pcb as <board>_2d_view.<ext>; filePath is returned. Use file mode for large boards to avoid MCP message-size limits.

pcbPath.description: Absolute path to the .kicad_pcb file. Falls back to the currently loaded board if omitted.

layers.description: Layer names to include, e.g. ["F.Cu","B.Cu","Edge.Cuts"]. Omit for all layers.

responseMode.description: "inline" (default): image returned directly; "file": written to disk, filePath returned

name: get_board_extents
description: Return the bounding box (min/max X and Y) of all objects on the current PCB board.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the result

name: get_board_info
description: Retrieve general information about the current PCB board (dimensions, layer count, DRC status).

name: get_category_tools
description: Return all tools available in a specific category. Use list_tool_categories first to find valid category names.

name: get_component_list
description: Return a list of all components on the PCB, optionally filtered by layer or bounding box region.

name: get_component_pads
description: Return all pads of a PCB component with their positions, net assignments and sizes.

name: get_component_properties
description: Return all properties of a PCB component (position, rotation, layer, value, footprint).

name: get_datasheet_url
description:
Get the LCSC datasheet URL for a component by LCSC number.

Returns the direct PDF URL and product page URL.
No network request – URL is constructed from the LCSC number alone.

Example: get_datasheet_url("C179739")
→ https://www.lcsc.com/datasheet/C179739.pdf

lcsc.description: LCSC part number, with or without "C" prefix (e.g. "C179739" or "179739")

name: get_design_rules
description: Return the current PCB design rules (clearance, track width, via sizes, courtyard settings).

name: get_drc_violations
description: Return the list of current DRC violations on the PCB, optionally filtered by severity (error, warning).

name: get_elements_in_region
description: List all symbols, wires, and labels within a rectangular region of the schematic. Useful for understanding what is in a specific area before modifying it.

x1.description: Left X coordinate of the region in mm

y1.description: Top Y coordinate of the region in mm

x2.description: Right X coordinate of the region in mm

y2.description: Bottom Y coordinate of the region in mm

name: get_footprint_info
description: Get detailed information about a specific footprint

library_name.description: Name of the library containing the footprint

footprint_name.description: Name of the footprint to get information about

name: get_jlcpcb_database_stats
description: Get statistics about the local JLCPCB parts database

name: get_jlcpcb_part
description: Get detailed information about a specific JLCPCB part by LCSC number

name: get_layer_list
description: Return the list of all layers defined in the current PCB board.

name: get_net_at_point
description: Returns the net name at a given (x, y) coordinate in a schematic, or null if no net label or wire endpoint is present at that position. Faster than get_pin_net when you only need the net name at a known coordinate and don't need pin traversal.

name: get_net_connections
description: Get all connections for a named net

netName.description: Name of the net to query

name: get_nets_list
description: Get a list of all nets in the PCB with optional statistics.

includeStats.description: Include statistics (track count, total length, etc.)

name: get_pad_position
description: Return the exact XY position of a specific pad on a PCB component. Use this before routing to get accurate start/end coordinates.

name: get_project_info
description: Get information about the current KiCAD project

name: get_schematic_component
description: Get full component info from a schematic: position, every field's value, and each field's label position (at x/y/angle). Returns ALL properties — both built-in fields (Reference, Value, Footprint, Datasheet) and any custom BOM/sourcing properties present on the symbol (MPN, Manufacturer, DigiKey_PN, LCSC, Voltage, Tolerance, Dielectric, etc.). Use this before edit_schematic_component / set_schematic_component_property to inspect what is currently set, or to plan a label repositioning.

name: get_schematic_pin_locations
description: Returns the exact x/y coordinates of every pin on a schematic component. Use this before add_schematic_net_label to place labels correctly on pin endpoints.

reference.description: Component reference designator (e.g. U1, R1, J2)

name: get_schematic_view
description: Return a rasterized image of the schematic (PNG by default, or SVG). Uses kicad-cli to export SVG, then converts to PNG via cairosvg. Use this for visual feedback after placing or wiring components.

name: get_schematic_view_region
description: Export a cropped region of the schematic as an image (PNG or SVG). Specify bounding box coordinates in schematic mm. Useful for zooming into a specific area to inspect wiring or layout.

x1.description: Left X coordinate of the region in mm

y1.description: Top Y coordinate of the region in mm

x2.description: Right X coordinate of the region in mm

y2.description: Bottom Y coordinate of the region in mm

name: get_symbol_info
description: Get detailed information about a specific symbol (global or project-scope when projectPath is supplied or a project has been opened).

projectPath.description: Optional: project directory or .kicad_pro/.kicad_pcb/.kicad_sch path so project-scope libraries are searched.

name: get_wire_connections
description: Returns the net name and all wires and component pins connected at a given point. Accepts either a component reference + pin number (e.g. reference='U1', pin='3') or a schematic coordinate (x, y in mm). Returns net=null for unnamed (unlabelled) nets. The query point must be at a wire endpoint or junction — midpoints are not matched. Use get_schematic_pin_locations or list_schematic_wires to obtain exact endpoint coordinates.

reference.description: Component reference (e.g. U1, R1). Pair with pin.

pin.description: Pin number or name (e.g. '3', 'SDA'). Pair with reference.

x.description: X coordinate of a wire endpoint in mm. Pair with y.

y.description: Y coordinate of a wire endpoint in mm. Pair with x.

name: group_components
description: Group multiple PCB components together by name for easier selection and manipulation.

references.description: Reference designators of components to group

name: import_3d_model
description: Copy a 3D model file (.step/.stp/.wrl/.x3d/.iges) into the project's *.3dshapes library folder and return a portable ${KIPRJMOD}/... path. Feed the returned 'modelPath' straight into add_footprint_3d_model or add_component_3d_model.

modelPath.description: Path to the source 3D model file to import

projectPath.description: Path to the .kicad_pro file or the project directory (used to locate the .3dshapes folder and compute ${KIPRJMOD})

libraryDir.description: Target *.3dshapes directory (absolute, or relative to the project). Default: <project>/<project>.3dshapes

newName.description: Rename the copied file (source extension kept if omitted)

overwrite.description: Overwrite an existing destination file (default false)

name: import_eagle_project
description: Import an Eagle project (.brd + .sch) and convert it to a KiCad project. Converts the PCB layout via kicad-cli and translates the schematic from Eagle XML to KiCad S-expression format.

board_file.description: Absolute path to the Eagle .brd board file

schematic_file.description: Absolute path to the Eagle .sch schematic file (auto-detected from .brd path if omitted)

output_dir.description: Output directory for the KiCad project (defaults to a subdirectory next to the .brd file)

project_name.description: KiCad project name (defaults to the .brd filename without extension)

name: import_ses
description: Import a Specctra SES (session) file into the current PCB. Use after running Freerouting externally.

sesPath.description: Path to the .ses file to import

name: import_svg_logo
description: Imports an SVG file as filled graphic polygons onto a KiCAD PCB layer (default F.SilkS / front silkscreen). Curves are linearised automatically. Ideal for placing a company or project logo on the board.

svgPath.description: Path to the SVG logo file

x.description: X position of the logo top-left corner in mm

y.description: Y position of the logo top-left corner in mm

width.description: Target width of the logo in mm (height is scaled to preserve aspect ratio)

layer.description: PCB layer name, e.g. F.SilkS or B.SilkS (default: F.SilkS)

strokeWidth.description: Outline stroke width in mm (0 = no outline, default 0)

filled.description: Fill polygons with solid colour (default true)

name: launch_kicad_ui
description: Launch KiCAD UI, optionally with a project file

projectPath.description: Optional path to .kicad_pcb file to open

autoLaunch.description: Whether to launch KiCAD if not running (default: true)

name: list_floating_labels
description: Returns all net labels in the schematic that are not connected to any component pin. A label is 'floating' when no component pin falls on the wire-network reachable from the label's position. Floating labels indicate misplaced or off-grid labels that cause ERC errors. Does not require the KiCAD UI to be running.

name: list_footprint_libraries
description: List available .pretty footprint libraries and their contents (first 20 footprints per library). Searches KiCAD standard install paths by default.

searchPaths.description: Override default search paths. Each entry should be a directory that contains .pretty subdirs.

name: list_libraries
description: List all available KiCAD footprint libraries

search_paths.description: Optional additional search paths for libraries

name: list_library_footprints
description: List all footprints in a specific KiCAD library

library_name.description: Name of the library to list footprints from

filter.description: Optional filter pattern for footprint names

limit.description: Maximum number of footprints to list

name: list_library_symbols
description: List all symbols in a specific KiCAD symbol library (global or project-scope when projectPath is supplied or a project has been opened).

projectPath.description: Optional: project directory or .kicad_pro/.kicad_pcb/.kicad_sch path to resolve project-scope libraries.

name: list_schematic_components
description: List all components in a schematic with their references, values, positions, and pins. Essential for inspecting what's on the schematic before making edits.

name: list_schematic_labels
description: List all net labels, global labels, and power flags in the schematic. Optionally filter by label name (netName) and/or label type (labelType).

netName.description: Filter to labels whose name exactly matches this string (case-sensitive). Omit to return all labels.

labelType.description: Filter by label type. 'net' = local label, 'global' = global label, 'power' = power symbol. Omit to return all types.

name: list_schematic_nets
description: List all nets in the schematic with their connections.

name: list_schematic_texts
description: List all free-form text annotations (notes, headings, documentation strings) in the schematic. Returns position, angle, font size, bold/italic flags, and justification for each text element. Optionally filter by a substring match on the text content.

text.description: Case-insensitive substring filter — only return texts containing this string

name: list_schematic_wires
description: List all wires in the schematic with start/end coordinates.

name: list_symbol_libraries
description: List all available KiCAD symbol libraries from global sym-lib-table, plus the project's sym-lib-table when projectPath (or any related file) is supplied or a project has been opened.

projectPath.description: Optional: project directory or .kicad_pro/.kicad_pcb/.kicad_sch path. Including this exposes project-scope sym-lib-table libraries.

name: list_symbol_pins
description: Return pin names, numbers, and types for a symbol directly from the library — no schematic required. Use this before add_schematic_component to discover pins for connect_to_net calls. Each pin has 'number' (e.g. '1', 'A5') and 'name' (e.g. 'FB', 'GND') — connect_to_net accepts either. Pass schematicPath to resolve project-local symbols. Returns close-match suggestions if the symbol name is slightly wrong.

schematicPath.description: Path to .kicad_sch — enables project-local sym-lib-table lookup

name: list_symbols_in_library
description: List all symbol names in a .kicad_sym library file.

name: list_tool_categories
description: List all available KiCAD tool categories with their descriptions and tool counts. Use this to discover which tools are available via the router.

name: modify_trace
description: Modify an existing trace (change width, layer, or net).

traceUuid.description: UUID of the trace to modify

name: move_component
description: Move a PCB component to a new position. Optionally update rotation or flip to a different copper layer.

position.description: New position coordinates and unit

layer.description: Optional target layer (e.g., 'F.Cu', 'B.Cu') - flips component if needed

name: move_schematic_component
description: Move a placed symbol to a new position in the schematic. By default (preserveWires=true) wire endpoints touching the component's pins are stretched to follow the new position.

position.description: New position in schematic mm coordinates

preserveWires.description: Stretch connected wire endpoints to follow the move (default true)

name: move_schematic_net_label
description: Move a net label (local, global, or hierarchical) to a new position in the schematic. Use currentPosition to disambiguate when multiple labels share the same name.

netName.description: Name of the net label to move

currentPosition.description: Current position to disambiguate when multiple labels share the same name

labelType.description: Restrict search to a specific label type

name: open_project
description: Open an existing KiCAD project

name: place_component
description: Place a footprint component onto the PCB at the specified position. Optionally set reference, value, footprint, rotation and layer.

componentId.description: Identifier for the component to place (e.g., 'R_0603_10k')

position.description: Position coordinates and unit

boardPath.description: Path to the .kicad_pcb file – required when using project-local footprint libraries

name: place_component_array
description: Place a rectangular grid array of identical components on the PCB with configurable row/column spacing.

name: query_traces
description: Query traces on the board with optional filters by net, layer, or bounding box.

name: query_zones
description: Query copper zones (filled pours) on the board with optional filters by net, layer, or bounding box. Returns zone net, layers, priority, fill state, and bounding box. Useful for auditing power planes and GND pours that query_traces does not include.

layer.description: Filter by layer name (matches zones that include this layer)

boundingBox.description: Filter to zones whose bounding box overlaps this region

name: refill_zones
description: Refill all copper zones on the board. WARNING: SWIG path has known segfault risk (see KNOWN_ISSUES.md). Prefer using IPC backend (KiCAD open) or triggering zone fill via KiCAD UI instead.

name: register_footprint_library
description: Register a .pretty footprint library in KiCAD's fp-lib-table so KiCAD can find the footprints. Run this after create_footprint when KiCAD shows 'library not found in footprint library table'.

libraryPath.description: Full path to the .pretty directory to register

libraryName.description: Nickname for the library in KiCAD (default: directory name without .pretty)

scope.description: project = writes fp-lib-table next to the .kicad_pro file (default); global = writes to the user's global KiCAD config

projectPath.description: Path to the .kicad_pro file or its directory (required for scope=project when the library is not in the project folder)

name: register_symbol_library
description: Register a .kicad_sym library in KiCAD's sym-lib-table so symbols can be used in schematics. Run this after create_symbol when KiCAD shows 'library not found'.

scope.description: project = writes sym-lib-table next to .kicad_pro; global = user config

projectPath.description: Path to .kicad_pro or its directory (for scope=project)

name: remove_schematic_component_property
description:
Remove a single custom property from a placed schematic symbol.

Built-in fields (Reference, Value, Footprint, Datasheet) cannot be removed —
KiCad requires them on every symbol. To clear a built-in field, use
edit_schematic_component and set its value to an empty string.

reference.description: Reference designator of the component (e.g. R1, U3)

name: replace_component
description: Replace an existing PCB component with a different component type, optionally changing footprint and value.

reference.description: Reference designator of the component to replace

newComponentId.description: ID of the new component to use

name: replace_schematic_component
description: Replace a placed component's symbol with a different one (e.g. Device:R -> Device:R_Potentiometer), preserving its position, rotation, and field values (Value/Footprint/custom). Optionally override rotation with newRotation. Returns the new pin positions.

reference.description: Reference of the component to replace (e.g., D1)

newRotation.description: Override rotation in degrees (default: keep existing)

name: rotate_component
description: Rotate a PCB component to an absolute angle in degrees.

angle.description: Rotation angle in degrees (absolute, not relative)

name: rotate_schematic_component
description: Rotate a placed symbol in the schematic.

angle.description: Absolute rotation in degrees (0, 90, 180, 270). This is the symbol's final orientation, not a relative increment — passing 90 sets the symbol to 90° regardless of its current angle (unlike KiCad's UI 'R' key).

name: route_arc_trace
description: Route a copper arc trace defined by start/mid/end points. Uses true PCB arc primitives when available.

name: route_differential_pair
description: Route a differential pair between two sets of points.

name: route_pad_to_pad
description: PREFERRED tool for pad-to-pad routing. Looks up pad positions automatically, detects the net from the pad, and — critically — if the two pads are on different copper layers (e.g. J1 on F.Cu and J2 on B.Cu) automatically inserts a via at the midpoint so the connection is complete. Always use this instead of route_trace when routing between named component pads.

fromPad.description: Pad number on the source component (e.g. '6' or 6)

toPad.description: Pad number on the target component (e.g. '15' or 15)

width.description: Trace width in mm (default: board default)

net.description: Net name override (default: auto-detected from pad)

name: route_trace
description: Route a trace segment between two XY points on a fixed layer. WARNING: Does NOT handle layer changes — if start and end are on different copper layers, use route_pad_to_pad instead, which automatically inserts a via.

name: run_drc
description: Run the KiCAD Design Rule Check (DRC) on the current PCB and return violations. Optionally save the report to a file.

reportPath.description: Optional path to save the DRC report

name: run_erc
description: Runs the KiCAD Electrical Rules Check (ERC) on a schematic and returns all violations. Use after wiring to verify the schematic before generating a netlist.

name: save_project
description: Save the current KiCAD project. Refuses to overwrite the board file if its contents changed on disk since load (external edit) unless force is true.

path.description: Optional new path to save to

force.description: Overwrite the loaded board file even if its on-disk contents changed externally

name: search_footprints
description: Search for footprints matching a pattern across all libraries

search_term.description: Search term or pattern to match footprint names

library.description: Optional specific library to search in

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: search_jlcpcb_parts
description:
Search JLCPCB parts catalog by specifications.

Searches the local JLCPCB database (must be downloaded first with download_jlcpcb_database).
Provides real pricing, stock info, and library type (Basic parts = free assembly).

Use this to find components with exact specifications and cost optimization.

library_type.description: Filter by library type (Basic = free assembly at JLCPCB)

in_stock.description: Only show parts with available stock

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: search_symbols
description:
Search for symbols in local KiCAD symbol libraries.

Searches by: symbol name, LCSC ID, description, manufacturer, MPN, category.
Use this to find components already in your local libraries (e.g., JLCPCB-KiCad-Library).

Returns symbol references that can be used directly in schematics.

library.description: Optional: filter to specific library name pattern (e.g., 'JLCPCB')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

projectPath.description: Optional: project directory or .kicad_pro/.kicad_pcb/.kicad_sch path so project-scope sym-lib-table libraries are searched too.

name: search_tools
description: Search all available KiCAD tools by keyword. Returns matching tool names and their categories.

name: set_active_layer
description: Set the currently active PCB layer by name (e.g. F.Cu, B.Cu).

layer.description: Layer name to set as active

name: set_board_size
description: Set the PCB board dimensions (width and height) in the specified unit.

name: set_design_rules
description: Configure PCB design rules: clearance, track width, via dimensions and courtyard requirements.

requireCourtyard.description: Whether to require courtyards for all footprints

name: set_footprint_type
description: Set the placement type (through_hole / smd / unspecified) and optional exclusion flags on a placed PCB footprint. The placement type controls whether the footprint is included in pick-and-place (.pos) output files. Use exclude_from_pos_files to suppress a footprint from .pos exports without changing its type.

type.description: Placement type: 'smd' for surface-mount, 'through_hole' for PTH components, 'unspecified' to clear both bits (e.g. for board-only or mechanically-placed items)

exclude_from_pos_files.description: When true, suppress this footprint from pick-and-place (.pos) exports. Omit to leave the current setting unchanged.

exclude_from_bom.description: When true, suppress this footprint from BoM exports. Omit to leave the current setting unchanged.

not_in_schematic.description: When true, marks the footprint as board-only (no corresponding schematic symbol). Omit to leave the current setting unchanged.

name: set_layer_constraints
description: Set per-layer design rule constraints (minimum track width, clearance and via dimensions).

minTrackWidth.description: Minimum track width for this layer (mm)

minViaDiameter.description: Minimum via diameter for this layer (mm)

minViaDrill.description: Minimum via drill size for this layer (mm)

name: set_schematic_component_property
description:
Add or update a single custom property on a placed schematic symbol.

This is a focused convenience wrapper around edit_schematic_component for the very
common case of attaching one BOM / sourcing field at a time. The property is
created if it does not already exist on the component.

Typical custom properties:
  • MPN, Manufacturer, Manufacturer_PN — manufacturer part number metadata
  • DigiKey, DigiKey_PN, Mouser_PN, LCSC, JLCPCB_PN — distributor part numbers
  • Voltage, Tolerance, Power, Dielectric, Temperature_Coefficient — passive parameters
  • Description, Notes — free-form documentation
  • Any custom field your BOM exporter expects.

These properties are written into the .kicad_sch file as standard KiCad property
records, are exported by export_bom, and are picked up by the JLCPCB and Digi-Key
sourcing tools. Newly-created properties default to hidden — set hide=false to
display the value on the schematic canvas.

For batch updates of multiple properties at once, use edit_schematic_component
with the `properties` parameter instead.

reference.description: Reference designator of the component (e.g. R1, U3)

value.description: Property value to write (use empty string to clear)

x.description: Label X position in mm (default: component X)

y.description: Label Y position in mm (default: component Y)

hide.description: Hide the property text on the schematic canvas. Defaults to true for newly-created custom properties.

fontSize.description: Font size in mm for the label (default: 1.27)

justify.description: Text justification for the property label. KiCad alignment keywords: "left", "right", "center", "top", "bottom", or combined e.g. "left top". Omit to leave unchanged. Pass "center" to reset to the KiCad default.

name: set_schematic_property_position
description: Move a component's Reference or Value field label to an absolute (x, y) coordinate (mm), optionally rotating or hiding it. Only 'Reference' and 'Value' are supported. Use autoplace_schematic_fields to place all of them automatically.

x.description: New X position in mm (absolute schematic coordinate)

y.description: New Y position in mm (absolute schematic coordinate)

visible.description: Whether the field is visible (default true)

name: snap_to_grid
description: Snap schematic element coordinates to the nearest grid point. KiCAD uses exact integer matching for connectivity, so off-grid coordinates cause wires that look connected to fail ERC checks. Modifies the .kicad_sch file in place. Does not require the KiCAD UI to be running.

gridSize.description: Grid spacing in mm (default: 2.54 — standard KiCAD schematic grid)

elements.description: Element types to snap (default: ["wires", "junctions", "labels"]). "components" is opt-in — moving a component without re-routing wires creates new mismatches.

name: snapshot_project
description: Save a named checkpoint snapshot of the current project state (renders board to PDF and records step label). Call after completing each major step — e.g. after Step 1 (schematic_ok) and Step 2 (layout_ok). Required by the demo workflow before waiting for user confirmation.

step.description: Step number or identifier, e.g. '1' or '2'

label.description: Short label for this checkpoint, e.g. 'schematic_ok' or 'layout_ok'

prompt.description: Full prompt text to save as PROMPT_step{step}_{timestamp}.md alongside the snapshot

name: suggest_jlcpcb_alternatives
description:
Suggest alternative JLCPCB parts for a given component.

Finds similar parts that may be cheaper, have more stock, or are Basic library type.
Useful for cost optimization and finding alternatives when parts are out of stock.

lcsc_number.description: Reference LCSC part number to find alternatives for

limit.description: Maximum number of alternatives to return

name: suggest_placement
description: Propose an optimized PCB footprint placement that shortens net length, orients parts toward their partners, and removes courtyard overlaps. Force-directed clustering pulls connected parts together (a converter's feedback divider and decoupling caps end up hugging its IC), power/high-current nets are weighted short & direct, and each part is rotated (0/90/180/270) to face neighbours so airwires stop crossing. PCB ONLY — does not touch the schematic. DRY RUN by default: returns proposals {ref:[x,y,rot]} plus a score (HPWL before/after, overlap counts) without modifying the board. Validate via check_courtyard_overlaps(positions=proposals), then re-run with apply=true before autoroute.

refs.description: References to move (default: every non-locked footprint on the board).

locked.description: References to hold fixed as anchors (connectors, mounting-constrained, RF, edge parts). They still pull movable parts. KiCad-locked footprints are added automatically.

apply.description: If true, move + rotate components to the proposed positions. Default false (dry run — board untouched).

grid_mm.description: Snap proposed positions to this grid (default 0.5).

spread.description: Enable density spreading (default true). Diffuses parts across free board area so a whole-board run stays legal (few/zero courtyard overlaps) instead of over-packing into a blob. Leave on for whole-board runs.

align.description: Tidy the result into rows/columns (default true). Snaps near-collinear part centers onto shared row (Y) and column (X) lines so passives line up cleanly with centers aligned — like KiCad's Align Centers + Distribute. Disable for a pure shortest-wire layout.

align_tol_mm.description: Max center spacing (mm) for parts to be pulled onto the same row/column line during align (default 1.5).

power_nets.description: Net-name fragments treated as high-current and pulled short & direct (case-insensitive). Defaults to common rails (VBAT, VBUS, VCC, 3V3, 5V, ...). Pass [] to disable.

decoupling_boost.description: Extra pull for 2-pin-passive <-> multi-pin-IC links so caps/feedback parts hug their IC (default 2.0).

bounds.description: SCOPED REGROUP: confine movable parts to this box (mm) — e.g. the area beside one IC. Combine with `refs` (that IC's passives) to regroup one cluster at a time; unlisted parts stay as anchors. Far more reliable than a whole-board run on a dense board. Default: whole board.

board_outline.description: Optional board containment bbox override. Default: derived from Edge.Cuts.

name: suggest_schematic_declutter
description: Re-orient overlapping net/global labels so their text lands in free space and becomes readable. Each label's (at x,y) anchor is its electrical connection point, so it is held FIXED — only the orientation (0/90/180/270) and justification change, throwing the text away from component bodies and other labels. Connectivity is never altered. DRY RUN by default: returns proposals [{name, at, from_angle, to_angle}] plus an overlap score (before/after) WITHOUT modifying the schematic. Set apply=true to rewrite the label orientations. (Phase 1: labels only; symbol spreading + wire reroute is a separate future capability.)

margin.description: Extra clearance in mm when testing label overlap (default 0.3).

references.description: Limit which component bodies count as obstacles (default: every component on the sheet).

apply.description: If true, rewrite the label orientations. Default false (dry run — schematic untouched, proposals only).

name: sync_schematic_to_board
description: Import the schematic netlist into the PCB board — equivalent to pressing F8 in KiCAD (Tools → Update PCB from Schematic). MUST be called after the schematic is complete and before placing or routing components on the PCB. Without this step, the board has no footprints and no net assignments — place_component and route_pad_to_pad will produce an empty, unroutable board.

schematicPath.description: Absolute path to the .kicad_sch schematic file

boardPath.description: Absolute path to the .kicad_pcb board file

name: update_symbol_from_library
description: Refresh embedded lib_symbols cache entries from a KiCad symbol library (equivalent to KiCad's Update Symbol from Library). Skips mirror-cache entries (__m0, __m90, …). Flattens (power) symbols for schematic format. Pass projectsDir to update all schematics in a folder, or schematicPath for one file.

projectsDir.description: Directory containing project subfolders with .kicad_sch files

libraryName.description: Symbol library nickname from sym-lib-table (e.g. Device, project_lib)

symbols.description: Optional: update only these symbol names (without Library: prefix)

repairMirrorFromBackup.description: Restore __m* mirror-cache lib_symbols blocks from backupDir first

backupDir.description: Backup folder with matching .kicad_sch filenames (for repairMirrorFromBackup)
